title: Hadrolaelia pumila
intro: Hadrolaelia pumila, also known as the miniature Hadrolaelia, is a rare and diminutive species of orchid native to Brazil. Traditionally, this plant is used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hadrolaelia pumila include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Hadrolaelia pumila is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, which is used to treat colds, flu, and other infections.
taxonomy: Hadrolaelia pumila is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on the ground, and is native to the Americas. The classification of Hadrolaelia pumila places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies of orchids. This subfamily is further divided into several tribes, with Hadrolaelia pumila being part of the tribe Cymbidieae. The exact classification of Hadrolaelia pumila may vary depending on the source and the current taxonomy.

Research on the plant's medicinal properties is still limited, and more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness.
